nameMeanings.name: Blundell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly Lancashire): from Old French blondel, a diminutive of blond ‘blond, fair’, used for someone with fair hair or a fair complexion and also used as a personal name. Compare Blunt .

variants.name: Blunt
meanings: English: nickname for someone with fair hair or a light complexion, from Old French blund, blond (from Latin blondus) ‘blond, fair, yellow-haired’, used also of complexion.

variants.name: Blondell
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Emile.
Swedish: ornamental name from blond ‘of light hair color’ or ‘of light complexion’ + the suffix -ell, a derivative of the Latin adjectival ending -elius.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William Blundell was born in 1410, in Bishop's Castle, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Richard Blundell, was 21 and his mother, Mrs Richard Blundell, was 21. He had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Eva Howell.
